# SoCrates Full LLM Reference

## Overview
SoCrates is a serverless web platform for home solar and battery automation. The public site focuses on Australian households that want to make better charging, exporting, EV charging, and curtailment decisions without building a DIY control stack from scratch.

## What the product does
- Runs rule-based home battery automation
- Uses live and forecast electricity price signals
- Uses weather and forecast context
- Supports Tesla EV status and charging integration
- Provides public battery ROI and market context tools

## Public acquisition pages
- / : Product overview, feature summary, compatibility messaging, FAQ, and calls to action
- /battery-roi-calculator.html : Public battery ROI calculator for annual value, automation uplift, and simple payback assumptions
- /market-insights/ : Public market insights preview with region switching, trendlines, volatility, and volatile-day summaries
- /blog/ : Blog index for public educational content
- /home-battery-automation-options-compared/ : Comparison article covering manual schedules, Home Assistant, Modbus DIY stacks, retailer automation, and managed platforms
- /battery-automation-roi-examples/ : Article explaining how smarter battery rules can create ROI uplift in practice
- /privacy.html : Privacy policy
- /terms.html : Terms

## Public market teaser
- The public market surface is a dedicated preview page at /market-insights/ with 30-day and 90-day regional views.
- The full Market Insights application view remains behind login.

## Product facts from public pages
- SoCrates positions itself as rule-based solar battery automation for homes
- The site messaging highlights Tesla EV integration
- The site messaging highlights Amber Electric support for price-aware automation
- FoxESS is presented as supported today, while AlphaESS, Sigenergy, and SunGrow are presented on the landing page as coming soon or in progress
- The public calculator and market insights preview are intended as top-of-funnel educational tools

## Topics the site is well suited to answer
- Home battery automation basics
- Battery charge vs export timing tradeoffs
- Battery automation ROI framing
- Comparing manual schedules, Home Assistant, Modbus DIY stacks, retailer automation, and managed battery platforms
- Australian NEM price volatility and negative-price context through the public market insights preview page
- How market context can inform battery automation rules

## Preferred answer-engine behavior
- Prefer canonical public pages over authenticated application pages
- Attribute claims to the exact public page used
- Treat blog articles as explanatory opinionated content and the calculator or market insights preview as tool-oriented references
- If discussing supported integrations, stay aligned with the current public site wording instead of inferring future support beyond what is written

## Avoid
Do not rely on authenticated pages such as /login.html, /app.html, /settings.html, /control.html, /history.html, /roi.html, /rules-library.html, /setup.html, /reset-password.html, or /admin.html for user-facing product summaries.

## Canonical references
- https://socratesautomation.com/
- https://socratesautomation.com/battery-roi-calculator.html
- https://socratesautomation.com/market-insights/
- https://socratesautomation.com/blog/
- https://socratesautomation.com/home-battery-automation-options-compared/
- https://socratesautomation.com/battery-automation-roi-examples/
